description: Person experienced serious delinquency within two years.
phase_1_note: Direct target field for Give Me Some Credit.

description: Credit card and unsecured line utilization.
expected_risk_direction: Higher utilization should generally mean higher risk.
phase_1_note: Values above 1 need review as extreme utilization/outlier behavior.

expected_risk_direction: Younger borrowers may carry higher risk; relationship can be nonlinear.
phase_1_note: Age 0 is invalid and must be handled in cleaning.

description: Count of 30-59 day delinquencies in the last two years.
expected_risk_direction: Higher count should mean higher risk.
phase_1_note: Values 96 and 98 are sentinel-like outliers to treat deliberately.

description: Monthly debt payments and living costs divided by monthly gross income.
expected_risk_direction: Higher ratio should generally mean higher risk.
phase_1_note: Mixed semantics: when MonthlyIncome is missing (~20% of rows) the field holds a raw dollar amount, not a ratio (median ~1159 vs ~0.30 when income is present), so it partly proxies the income-missing population. Capping does not fix this.

expected_risk_direction: Higher income should generally mean lower risk.
phase_1_note: Missing values should likely be retained as a WoE bin, not blindly imputed.

description: Open installment loans and credit lines.
expected_risk_direction: Ambiguous/nonlinear; too few or too many lines may be risky.
phase_1_note: Review binning manually rather than forcing a simple direction.

expected_risk_direction: Higher count should mean higher risk.
phase_1_note: Values 96 and 98 are sentinel-like outliers to treat deliberately.

description: Mortgage and real estate loan count, including home equity lines.
expected_risk_direction: Ambiguous/nonlinear; moderate secured credit can be lower risk.

description: Count of 60-89 day delinquencies in the last two years.
expected_risk_direction: Higher count should mean higher risk.
phase_1_note: Values 96 and 98 are sentinel-like outliers to treat deliberately.

description: Number of dependents in family excluding the borrower.
expected_risk_direction: More dependents may mean higher obligations, but effect is often weak.
phase_1_note: Missing values should be reviewed and likely kept as a bin.
